ideas.physics_build_review: Flow boiling above 500 W/cm2 hotspot flux is at the edge of published two-phase art but not beyond it; the subatmospheric variant is physically coherent (vacuum setpoint fixes saturation temperature, leaks pull air inward) yet inherits the classic low-pressure penalty of huge vapor specific volume, so condenser/line sizing and chugging-type flow instability at 2-5 kW/chip are the real engineering risks, and ITRI-class results (L14-053) are lab-scale only. TRL 4 is honest. The 2026-2029 path (fluid matrix with a chemical partner, 2027 2 kW demo at $400k, 2028 reliability run) is executable pre-company only with a committed fluid-maker partner, because the core risk - a qualified non-PFAS two-phase fluid existing at datacenter scale by 2031 - is chemistry the team does not control; their own kill trigger correctly encodes this. Capex is modest until productization ($10-25M v1); service burden of sealed loops in racks is real but bounded by vacuum-decay telemetry. Hidden showstopper: single-phase water simply winning the 3-5 kW generation, which several $100M-funded competitors are betting on.

G1.rationale: Two enacted, independent, chemistry-specific regulatory triggers with primary text (EPA AIM Act step-down L14-033 T1; EU F-gas 2024/573 L14-034), plus CN PUE mandates (L14-035, P3R2-C-04-S02). CN caveat: efficiency-driven, not chemistry-driven, so the PFAS-free wedge carries less force there.

G2.rationale: Technical claims rest on peer-reviewed two-phase reviews (L14-001/002/003, L14-022) and standards/regulator documents; no unreviewed academic claim is load-bearing.

G3.rationale: 2027 2 kW two-fluid demo plus 1,000 h sealed-loop assay is bounded and budgeted ($400k), though above the rubric's preferred $25-250k band.

G4.rationale: ZutaCore, Accelsius, LiquidStack/Trane, JetCool/Flex, Envicool named; the fluid-agnostic/negative-pressure wedge is real but rests on an absence-of-evidence finding (P3R2-C-04-S01: no competitor confirmed PFAS-free). One funded incumbent announcing a non-PFAS qualification dataset flips this gate.

G5.rationale: No new physics; triggers are enacted law, not forecasts; the fluid-availability dependency is honestly gated by the binding 2031 kill trigger rather than assumed away.

G6.rationale: Thermal hardware is uncontrolled; CN localization separable; fluid-chemistry trade rules flagged as pre-launch diligence. The unconfirmed CN 100% liquid-cooling mandate is correctly excluded per the binding condition.

G7.rationale: Best-anchored timing in batch: AIM Act 30% step 2029-2033 and EU 2030 placing-on-market ban are dated primary triggers landing at launch; bounded 2026-2029 plan; window-risk (single-phase wins if no fluid by 2031) explicitly analyzed.

demonstrated_demand.evidence: Regulatory compulsion is primary and dated (L14-033, L14-034, P3R2-C-04-S02) and Vertiv-class backlog shows liquid-cooling spend (L14-048), but no named buyer is yet tendering PFAS-free two-phase specifically; two-phase share by 2030 is an explicit 2-5% assumption in the evidence file.

frontier_coolness_vision.evidence: Owning the two-phase thermal layer for 5 kW chips with regulatory shocks as upgrade cycles is a genuine frontier narrative (L14-010, L14-053).

high_end_niche_quality.evidence: Drop-in >2 kW/chip module for ODM/CDU design-in is narrow and valuable (L14-043), but design-in access for a pre-revenue entrant against qualified vendor lists is hard.

competition_whitespace.evidence: Well-funded field: ZutaCore $100M+ (L14-046), Accelsius $65M (P3R2-C-04-S01), Trane and Flex acquisitions (L14-051, L14-045), Envicool/Huawei in CN; wedge is narrow positioning, not open space.

reachable_validation_budget.evidence: First experiment $400k exceeds the $250k band and v1 needs $10-25M, well past the $5M sellable-v1 preference.

technical_elegance_controllability.evidence: Saturation temperature set by vacuum setpoint is an elegant, measurable control variable; loop is benchtop-testable in bounded steps (L14-001/002/003, L14-053).

tenx_technical_edge.evidence: 500 W/cm2 hotspot flux is roughly 2-5x single-phase cold plates, plus a qualitative leak-inward safety property; not a clean order of magnitude.

us_china_dual_market_leverage.evidence: US leg strong and chemistry-specific (L14-033); CN leg real but efficiency-driven (L14-035, L14-039), so the differentiator weakens there per the evidence file's own verdict.

launch_window_fit_2030.evidence: AIM Act 2029-2033 step and EU 2030 ban straddle the launch year; window persists to mid-2030s (85% cut 2036); binding 2031 fluid kill trigger bounds the downside.

expansion_economics.evidence: Same loop family serves power electronics, EV fast-charging, radar; fluid-menu dataset compounds across chemistries.

founder_skill_transfer.evidence: founder_fit_note: sensing/vacuum-decay/loop-control electronics fit; core two-phase thermal-fluids and chemistry are outside the profile.

ideas.uncertainty_notes: TAM arithmetic in the evidence file is explicitly placeholder ($400-800/kW, 2-5% adoption assumptions); CN chemistry-specific demand unproven; competitor PFAS-free positioning is an absence-of-evidence finding; mechanism demo (negative-pressure loop at 2 kW) not yet run.
redteam_flags_for_p5: Attack the 2031 fluid-availability assumption: name the actual candidate non-PFAS two-phase fluids and their toxicity/GWP/qualification status
Stress the subatmospheric-water variant: vapor volumetric flow, condenser size, and dielectric risk at 2-5 kW/chip in a rack
Test whether ZutaCore/Accelsius fluids are quietly non-PFAS already, which would erase the wedge
Pressure-test CN demand: would a Chinese buyer pay anything for PFAS-free specifically given GB 40879 is chemistry-agnostic?

ideas.physics_build_review: Nothing here strains physics: programmable heater arrays, vapor-chamber test vehicles, and traceable calorimetry are established instrument engineering, and TRL 5 is credible given commercial component-level TTVs already exist (Taiwan PTC/KLC class, per evidence). The genuinely hard specs are 1,000 W/cm2-class transient hotspot emulation (needs thin-film heaters on silicon with fast drive, at the edge of heater art) and <=1% energy-balance closure at rack/2 MW scale (demanding but standard metrology discipline). The 2026-2029 path - 1.2 kW vehicle in a university lab for $150k, open cold-plate round-robin, rack-emulator pilot - is among the most executable in the batch, with tiny capex, low service burden, and a decisive cheap first experiment. Main showstopper risk is not technical: OCP or a certification lab shipping a full reference method before the company exists would demote the product to an implementation of someone else's standard.

G1.rationale: US: Google-authored Deschutes CDU spec with eight named vendor implementers (P3R2-C-05-S01 buyer filing, S02 cross-check) is primary buyer-side evidence of spec chaos without a conformance standard. CN: GB 40879/T-CIEP 0263 mandates (L14-035, L14-039) plus an identified CAICT/Taier certification channel.

G2.rationale: Claims rest on standards documents, buyer specs, and company filings; peer-reviewed thermal sources (L14-009/010) used only for background.

G3.rationale: Q4 2026-2027 1.2 kW programmable vehicle plus blind three-cold-plate round-robin at $150k is bounded, decisive, and inside the budget band.

G4.rationale: Closest competitors named and checked: component TTV vendors, in-house rigs, NTT/ITRI institutional analogs (L14-050, L14-053); no merchant rack-scale vendor found. Difference (traceable rack-scale calorimetry plus clause-scripted conformance) is non-cosmetic.

G5.rationale: No exotic physics; TAM ceiling honestly flagged ($25-135M hardware) rather than propped by one forecast.

G6.rationale: Test equipment is broadly uncontrolled; dual-entity structure trivial per evidence; no contrary finding.

G7.rationale: Timing sources exist (GB 40879 revision cadence primary; Deschutes buyer cycle), but the evidence file's core finding is that the window is ALREADY open 2026-2028 - the risk for a 2030 launch is arriving after the method authority is claimed, mitigated only if the 2026-27 university-lab methodology work actually establishes priority.

demonstrated_demand.evidence: A named buyer (Google) authored a hardware spec that eight named vendors build to with no acceptance-test standard behind it (P3R2-C-05-S01/S02); CN mandates create conformance obligations (L14-035, L14-039); Vertiv $15B backlog context (L14-048).

frontier_coolness_vision.evidence: 1,000 W/cm2 transient emulation with traceable calorimetry is technically sharp, but a metrology/picks-and-shovels vision is inherently mid-scale excitement.

high_end_niche_quality.evidence: 15-30 identifiable buyer organizations (Deschutes vendor list, CN certifiers), each needing benches; underserved per this session's merchant-vendor check.

competition_whitespace.evidence: No dominant merchant vendor found (evidence Section 2); institutional analogs only (L14-050, L14-053); main structural threat is the OCP standards process, not a company.

reachable_validation_budget.evidence: $150k decisive first experiment; v1 capital $3-8M straddles the $5M bar.

technical_elegance_controllability.evidence: Fully measurable instrument product with calibration chains and bounded engineering steps; 1% closure at 2 MW is hard but quantifiable.

tenx_technical_edge.evidence: Metrology fidelity nobody currently sells (transient hotspot maps plus traceable calorimetry), but versus in-house dummy loads the edge is fidelity/comparability, not an order-of-magnitude customer metric.

us_china_dual_market_leverage.evidence: Independent legs: US buyer-spec ecosystem (S01/S02) and CN mandatory standards with a named certification-lab channel (L14-035, L14-039, CAICT/Taier).

launch_window_fit_2030.evidence: Window already live 2026-2028 per evidence Section 6; durable past 2030 as TDP outpaces standards, but first-mover method authority may be claimed before a 2030 launch.

expansion_economics.evidence: Consumables, protocol licensing, post-PFAS fluid-qual rigs (A-15 merge); TAM ceiling honestly modest.

founder_skill_transfer.evidence: founder_fit_note: instrumentation, DAQ, controls, numerical analysis - core skill set.

ideas.uncertainty_notes: Bench pricing and TAM are labeled assumptions ($50-150k vehicles, $1-3M emulators, $25-135M cumulative); licensing/consumables economics unproven; OCP cold-plate qualification white paper could not be fetched (403) so its scope is unverified.
redteam_flags_for_p5: Fetch and read the OCP Cold Plate Development and Qualification white paper: if it already specifies a full method plus reference hardware, the wedge collapses
Verify CAICT/Taier's existing CDU bench capability - if they already build test hardware in-house, the CN channel is a competitor
Attack venture-scalability: can protocol licensing realistically lift a $25-135M hardware ceiling to a fundable company?
Test whether 2030 launch is simply too late - would 2027-2028 incumbent instrument entrants (Chroma-class) own the method by then?

ideas.physics_build_review: The physics is settled - ripple-dependent specific-energy penalties of 5-14% for six-pulse thyristor supplies are peer-reviewed (L11-016/017/019/020/021) and IGBT/AFE rectification at 5-50 MW is shipping product (Ingeteam INGECON, P3R2-C-07-S02) - so TRL 5 is honest, and that is precisely the problem: the technology is not scarce, the economics are. The evidence file's own bottom-up arithmetic yields a 6-12 year simple payback at conservative midpoints against a ~4-5x IGBT-vs-thyristor capex premium (unverified ne21.com figure), meaning the product only sells via performance-contract structures whose M&V burden lands on a small team. The 2026-2029 plan (ripple-vs-degradation study, $250k side-by-side SEC experiment) is executable and genuinely decisive. Service burden is heavy: MW-scale plant retrofits, per-cell telemetry, 15-20 year opex contracts. Hidden showstoppers: retrofit targets are either old plants owned by cost-minimizers or young plants under OEM warranty; and Sungrow occupies the CN lane as both largest buyer and vertically integrated competitor.

G1.rationale: Primary sources exist on both legs (DOE H2Hubs award L11-032; CN tenders L11-050/051; Kuqa underperformance L11-049), but they evidence electrolyzer-plant demand, not rectifier-retrofit demand; the policy-independent electrowinning beachhead has no named tender and is explicitly UNCERTAIN in the evidence file.

G2.rationale: The 5-14% penalty claims are peer-reviewed (L11-016 through L11-021, L11-026/027); no preprint is load-bearing.

G3.rationale: 2027 side-by-side SEC measurement under thyristor vs AFE supply, $250k, publishes a verified savings band - bounded and decisive.

G4.rationale: P4 gap now filled: Ingeteam ships an AFE electrolyzer rectifier today (S02) and ABB UNIREC exists (discovery-only); differentiation narrows to retrofit-of-installed-base plus stack-neutral M&V contracts - real but thin, and Sungrow verticalization is confirmed as the CN market leader's observed model.

G5.rationale: No physics risk; the marginal-payback problem is an economics risk handled by the performance-contract model and the 2033 kill trigger, not a single unverifiable forecast.

G6.rationale: Industrial rectifiers uncontrolled; 45V explicitly FEOC-exempt per S01; separability plan credible.

G7.rationale: The OBBBA 45V construction-start deadline of 2027-12-31 (S01, primary) is a NARROWING trigger: subsidized US new-build gets pulled before launch. The retrofit-installed-base thesis and CN second wave keep a 2030 window arguable, but the US hydrogen leg the seed leaned on is compressed, and the electrowinning substitute leg is unverified.

demonstrated_demand.evidence: No named buyer for retrofits on either leg; H2Hubs (L11-032) and CN tenders (L11-050/051) fund plants, not power-quality upgrades; electrowinning beachhead unverified this session (evidence Section 1).

frontier_coolness_vision.evidence: Precision-DC-as-a-product is a sound industrial thesis but AFE rectification is commodity power electronics; low frontier excitement.

high_end_niche_quality.evidence: Retrofit-plus-M&V niche is identifiable (Kuqa-class underperformers, L11-049; tankhouses per L11-045) but payback arithmetic (6-12 yr, evidence Section 3) makes the value proposition marginal.

competition_whitespace.evidence: Ingeteam shipping AFE units since 2023 (S02); ABB/Siemens lines; Sungrow verticalized in CN (evidence Section 2) - incumbents are neither few nor slow.

reachable_validation_budget.evidence: $250k decisive experiment at the band edge; v1 $8-18M exceeds $5M.

technical_elegance_controllability.evidence: Ripple synthesis, SEC measurement, and M&V telemetry are cleanly measurable; degradation-weighted scheduling is modellable (L11-004/012/013/031).

tenx_technical_edge.evidence: 5-14% energy saving is the whole prize (L11-016/021); sub-1% ripple at 100 kA is engineering polish on a single-digit-percent customer value.

us_china_dual_market_leverage.evidence: US leg time-compressed (45V 2027, S01) with unverified electrowinning fallback; CN leg real but its largest counterparty (Sungrow) is the kill-condition competitor (L11-051, evidence Section 2).

launch_window_fit_2030.evidence: Retrofit-base logic keeps a window arguable post-2030, but the subsidized US wave front-runs the launch and AFE new-build is already commoditizing (Ingeteam, Sungrow).

expansion_economics.evidence: Chlor-alkali, battery formation, electrowinning adjacencies are real if the wedge holds.

founder_skill_transfer.evidence: founder_fit_note: ripple control and M&V telemetry fit; MW rectifier hardware and plant electrochemistry do not.

ideas.uncertainty_notes: Payback arithmetic rests entirely on labeled assumptions (electricity $0.04/kWh, 8% saving, $150-300/kW retrofit premium); the 5x IGBT price premium is an unfetched trade-press figure; ABB product specs are discovery-only; electrowinning beachhead unsourced.
redteam_flags_for_p5: Rebuild the payback model with real plant data: if simple payback exceeds ~5 years at realistic power prices, the retrofit product is unsellable without exotic financing
Find one named electrowinning tankhouse rectifier tender or capex line - if none exists, the policy-independent beachhead is fictional
Test whether stack OEMs (Sungrow, Plug, Siemens Energy) simply bundle AFE supplies in new-build, leaving no retrofit-aged fleet by 2032
Verify the RMB200-300k vs RMB1.2-1.5M thyristor/IGBT price gap against a fetched primary source

ideas.physics_build_review: No new physics: diffusion-bonded PCHEs are commercial (Heatric 30+ years, VPE in the US per P3R2-C-08-S04); the frontier is the coupled creep-fatigue-corrosion problem at 500-700 C / 20+ MPa under >20 C/min transients, which Sandia documents as unresolved (L04-113) and CAS-IET actively researches (L04-029). Graded channels and compliant headers are plausible strain-management mechanisms but unproven at bond planes over 20-year life - the honest TRL for the rapid-transient-qualified variant is 3-4 against the claimed 5 (generic PCHE art is mature; the differentiating variant is not). The 2026-2029 path (university plus national-lab bonding work, $600k 1,000-cycle rig in 2028) is executable but capital-heavy for a pre-company team, and production requires vacuum hot presses and code-qualified QA - v1 $15-35M is the batch's heaviest. ASME Section III Div 5 code-case work is a multi-year burden the plan correctly starts in 2028. Showstopper watch: incumbents own the bonding capability and could qualify transient tolerance faster than a startup can build a factory.

G1.rationale: Strong on both legs: NRC Part 53 in force 2026-04-29 (S01, primary regulatory unlock), STEP Demo operational (L04-039), TerraPower BOP chain (L04-045); CN: Chaotan One commercially operating with both units (S03) plus the dated 2028 Xinjiang molten-salt+sCO2 project (S02).

G2.rationale: Technical base is peer-reviewed/standards-grade (L04-029, L04-112/113, ASTM E139); the ~80% recuperator capex share is literature-confirmed per evidence.

G3.rationale: 2028 subscale core with 1,000 rapid cycles at 550 C peaks, post-test sectioning, $600k - bounded and decisive, though above the preferred budget band.

G4.rationale: P4 gap filled: Heatric and VPE (S04) confirmed as real incumbents; neither publishes a rapid-transient-qualified line, so the wedge is plausible but unproven - neither vendor's transient specs were actually checked, which is exactly what would flip this gate.

G5.rationale: The single-source RMB100bn CN TAM is correctly held as a flagged scenario ceiling, not a base case; demand legs stand on operating plants and enacted regulation.

G6.rationale: Non-nuclear PCHEs uncontrolled; nuclear line kept US-entity; CNNC 1260H/NS-CMIC investment-list flags identified and correctly scoped to deal structuring, not component sales.

G7.rationale: Dated triggers on both legs (Part 53 effective 2026; Xinjiang completion 2028; first-set follow-on procurement into the 2030s), bounded 2026-2029 plan, kill trigger 2033 if no >=10 MW-class merchant order; commoditization before 2030 implausible in a qualified-vendor-constrained category.

demonstrated_demand.evidence: Operating reference plants and regulatory unlocks are real (S01, S03, L04-039), but no merchant order for a transient-qualified core exists, CNNC is vertically integrated on its flagships, and Chaotan One's rotor/HX sourcing model is unknown.

frontier_coolness_vision.evidence: Simultaneous creep-fatigue-corrosion frontier at 700 C/20 MPa (L04-113) with an Intel-of-heat-exchange platform vision across sCO2, heat pumps, hydrogen precooling.

high_end_niche_quality.evidence: Recuperators are ~80% of cycle capex and the most-cited bottleneck (L04-039, L04-029); qualified-vendor pool is tiny (L04-045).

competition_whitespace.evidence: Heatric/VPE/Chart are real and capable (S04), but no one occupies the rapid-transient-qualified niche on the public record this session.

reachable_validation_budget.evidence: $600k first experiment and $15-35M v1 are the heaviest in the advancing set; bonding infrastructure is capital-intensive.

technical_elegance_controllability.evidence: Cycling rigs and sectioning give measurable feedback (ASTM E139, L04-112), but 20-year creep-fatigue life extrapolation from 1,000-cycle tests is inherently loose.

tenx_technical_edge.evidence: >20 C/min qualified transient tolerance vs conventional PCHE ramp limits of a few C/min is an order-of-magnitude, customer-valued edge if demonstrated (L04-029 target).

us_china_dual_market_leverage.evidence: Genuinely independent legs: US Part 53/STEP lineage vs CN operating fleet plus 2028 first-set project (S01/S02/S03); CN served via licensed entity.

launch_window_fit_2030.evidence: Equipment-ordering phases land 2030-2034 on both legs; window opened by dated 2026/2028 triggers and unlikely to close early given code-qualification barriers.

expansion_economics.evidence: Catalog cores across sCO2, A-CAES, heat pumps, hydrogen; structural pairing with C-19 shares buyers and test infrastructure.

founder_skill_transfer.evidence: founder_fit_note: low direct fit; only test-loop instrumentation transfers.

ideas.uncertainty_notes: CN TAM single-source (state-media forecast); Heatric/VPE transient specs unchecked; L04-051 citation mismatch for the Xinjiang claim (corroborated but needs a new atlas source); TRL for the differentiating variant closer to 3-4 than the claimed 5.
redteam_flags_for_p5: Check Heatric's and VPE's actual published/quotable thermal-transient tolerances - if either already meets 20 C/min, the wedge is gone
Attack the 20-year bond-plane life claim: what accelerated-test protocol could credibly support it by 2030?
Triangulate the CN sintering retrofit fleet size from an eligible non-state-media source or strike the RMB100bn ceiling
Estimate real capex to first certified production core (vacuum hot press, code QA) - is $15-35M v1 honest?
